description: List of materia medica, in Arabic with Greek/Coptic numerals. Isaacs (1994) suggests that it presents a ‘diet for invalids’ but does not explain why. Also includes firewood and fuel, and in Judaeo-Arabic upside down, myrtle. On verso there are names such Abū Saʿd, Ṭāhir, Abū l-Faraj, and [...] b. al-Thiqa. Dating: Probably ca. 1150–1250. (Information in part from CUDL)
description_he: רשימה או טבלה בArabic, Greek/Coptic Numerals. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
